title: HoneyLabs Threat Intelligence (TAXII)
descriptionMarkdown: HoneyLabs runs internet-facing honeypot sensors and publishes the resulting indicators over TAXII 2.1: source IPs observed running exploit or loader commands, and the malware infrastructure (loader and C2 URLs) extracted from captured payloads. Indicators are evidence-backed, exclude known research scanners, and expire on their own via valid_until. This connector uses Microsoft Sentinel's built-in **Threat Intelligence - TAXII** data connector; the steps below give you the values to paste in. A free HoneyLabs API key is the only prerequisite.

sampleQueries.query: ThreatIntelligenceIndicator | where SourceSystem startswith "HoneyLabs" | where Active == true and ExpirationDateTime > now() | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by IndicatorId
ThreatIntelligenceIndicator | where SourceSystem startswith "HoneyLabs" | summarize count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)

sampleQueries.description: Exploiter IPs seen in your firewall logs
sampleQueries.query: ThreatIntelligenceIndicator | where SourceSystem startswith "HoneyLabs" and isnotempty(NetworkIP) | join kind=innerunique (CommonSecurityLog | where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)) on $left.NetworkIP == $right.SourceIP | summarize count() by NetworkIP

value: ThreatIntelligenceIndicator | where SourceSystem startswith "HoneyLabs" | summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated) | project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(7d)

dataTypes.name: ThreatIntelligenceIndicator
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery: ThreatIntelligenceIndicator | where SourceSystem startswith "HoneyLabs" | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated) | where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: read and write permissions are required.

customs.name: HoneyLabs API key
customs.description: A free HoneyLabs API key, created at [honeylabs.net/dashboard?src=sentinel](https://honeylabs.net/dashboard?src=sentinel). The key is used as the HTTP Basic password when Sentinel polls the TAXII server.

instructionSteps.title: 1. Get a HoneyLabs API key
instructionSteps.description: Sign in at [honeylabs.net/dashboard?src=sentinel](https://honeylabs.net/dashboard?src=sentinel) (accounts are free), open **API keys**, and create a key. Any HoneyLabs key works; the account it belongs to is what shows up in your HoneyLabs usage view.

instructionSteps.title: 2. Install the Threat Intelligence solution
instructionSteps.description: If your workspace does not have the **Threat Intelligence - TAXII** data connector yet, install the **Threat Intelligence** solution by Microsoft from the Content hub.

instructionSteps.title: 3. Connect the exploiters collection
instructionSteps.description: Open the **Threat Intelligence - TAXII** connector page and add a server with these values. The username can be any string; only the password (your API key) is checked. Hourly polling matches how often the collections change.

instructionSteps.title: 4. Connect the malware infrastructure collection
instructionSteps.description: Repeat the previous step with a second server entry for the loader and C2 URLs.

instructionSteps.title: 5. Verify
instructionSteps.description: Within a few minutes of the first poll, indicators appear in the **Threat intelligence** blade and in the ThreatIntelligenceIndicator table tagged with the friendly names above. Each indicator links back to its full HoneyLabs report (first seen, payloads, CVEs probed) at honeylabs.net/lookup.
